steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The student wants to "describe the process" of relief printing. A process description must explain the sequential mechanics of how something works — step by step — not background details, materials lists, or an artist's biography.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'Relief printing is a technique that can use wood, rubber, and other materials as the printing block, not just linoleum tile.': Lists materials only. No sequence of steps is described. ✗
  • Choice B — 'Catlett used a relief printing technique called lino cutting, carving an image onto linoleum tile.': Mentions only the carving step and frames it around the artist rather than the process itself. Incomplete process, wrong focus. ✗
  • Choice C — 'Catlett used relief printing to create the 1968 linocut Bread.': States a fact about the artwork with no description of how relief printing works. ✗
  • Choice D — 'In relief printing, an image is carved onto a printing block, which is then painted and pressed onto a print surface.': Walks through all three steps in order — carve, paint, press — without detailing the artist or materials. Directly describes the process. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
